ec2Enhancement.serviceDescription: Amazon EC2 provides a wide selection of instance types optimized to fit different use cases. Instance types comprise varying combinations of CPU, memory, storage, and networking capacity and give you the flexibility to choose the appropriate mix of resources for your applications.

cards.title: EC2 specifications
cards.helpText: An Amazon Machine Image (AMI) is a template that contains the software configuration (operating system, application server, and applications) required to launch an instance. AMIs are preconfigured with an ever-growing list of operating system. We work with our partners and community to provide you with the most choice possible. You are also empowered to use our bundling tools to upload your own operating systems.

components.label: Tenancy
components.description: Choose the tenancy type to run your Amazon EC2 instances on.

components.description: Choose the operating system to run your Amazon EC2 instances on.

components.fieldDescription: Choose the graph that best represents your monthly workload
components.label: Advance workload selection

radioOptions.label: Constant usage
radioOptions.description: This workload is suitable for use cases that have a constant, predicable load. This includes use cases such as logging traffic to a website or running processes in the background.

radioOptions.label: Daily spike traffic
radioOptions.description: This workload is best for usage patterns that peak once a day. This is suitable for scenarios where, for example, you need to run several jobs at midnight or have a morning news spike.

radioOptions.label: Weekly spike traffic
radioOptions.description: This workload is best for patterns that peak once a week. This is suitable for scenarios such as blogs that post once a week and weekly television shows.

radioOptions.label: Monthly spike traffic
radioOptions.description: This workload is best for traffic that spikes once a month, such as monthly invoices, payroll, or other monthly reports.

options.label: Compute Savings Plans
options.description: One plan that automatically applies to all usage on EC2, Fargate, and Lambda. Up to 66% discount. Learn more

options.label: EC2 Instance Savings Plans
options.description: Get deeper discount when you only need one instance family and region. Up to 72% discount. Learn more

options.label: On-Demand
options.description: Maximize flexibility. Learn more

options.label: Spot Instances
options.description: Minimize cost by leveraging EC2's spare capacity. Recommended for fault tolerant and interruption tolerant applications. Learn more

options.label: Convertible Reserved Instances
options.description: Learn more

cards.title: Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) - optional
cards.description: Attach persistent block storage volumes for your Amazon EC2 instances
cards.helpText: Amazon Elastic Block Store (EBS) allows you to create storage volumes and attach them to Amazon EC2 instances. Once attached, you can create a file system on top of these volumes, run a database, or use them in any other way you would use block storage.

components.content: Learn more on how EBS snapshot prices are calculated.

cards.title: Detailed monitoring - optional
cards.helpText: By default, your instance is enabled for basic monitoring. You can optionally enable detailed monitoring. Check the box if you want to enable Detailed Monitoring (at 1-minute frequency) on these instances.

components.label: Enable monitoring
components.description: Check the box if you want to enable Detailed Monitoring (at 1-minute frequency) on these instances.
